beats.prompt: Two new patterns, both length 3: THH and HTH. One waits longer on a fair coin. Which?

THH waits longer.note: Close, but it's the other way — let's build both and see.

HTH waits longer.note: Good hunch — HTH waits 10 vs THH's 8. We'll show why.

Same — both length 3.note: Same length, but not the same wait — structure decides it.

hints: No wrong guess yet — commit and we'll derive both.
Length is equal; self-overlap is not.
HTH waits longer (10 vs 8).

beats.prompt: Lock in the pair we'll work with.

feedback.correct: THH vs HTH — let's build them from scratch.
hints: Both length 3.
We'll derive each wait.
Continue.

beats.prompt: One word before we slide things around.

interaction.title: Overlap = self-similarity
interaction.body: A pattern "overlaps itself" when its start also appears at its end. Those overlaps are the only reason two same-length patterns can wait different amounts. We'll find them by sliding the pattern over itself.

hints: Start = end?
That's a self-overlap.
Continue.

beats.prompt: Flip until THH appears. Feel the deeper, 4-state graph — and watch a near-miss.

feedback.correct: Notice how a stray flip can knock you back a step, not all the way.
hints: Flip and watch the chip climb ∅ → T → TH → THH.
A bad flip falls back to a shorter match, not always to ∅.
Continue once THH lands.

beats.prompt: Slide HTH over itself. Where does the start reappear at the end? Each overlap adds a 2^L term.
beats.interviewNote: Sliding a pattern over itself is the autocorrelation / Guibas–Odlyzko correlation set; the border lengths are exactly the overlaps that set the expected wait.

feedback.correct: HTH overlaps at lengths 1 and 3 — that extra length-1 overlap is the source of the extra flips.
hints: Check each shift for a start-equals-end overlap.
HTH matches at length 3 (itself) and length 1 (the H).

beats.prompt: On the THH graph: you've matched TH. The next flip is make-or-break. Where does each outcome send you?

feedback.correct: Right — from TH, an H completes THH, but a T falls back to E1 (you still have a T), not all the way to ∅.
hints: From TH, which flip finishes THH?
A tail isn't a total reset here — you still hold a T.
E2 on H → done; E2 on T → E1.

beats.prompt: Build the wait-time system for THH. E₀ is worked; complete E₁ and E₂.

copy.workedExplanation: From E₀ (no progress) one flip costs 1. Heads leaves you at E₀ (THH needs a T first); tails advances to E₁ (you've matched the T).

wrong-var-generic: From a matched prefix, heads and tails lead to different states — trace each.
One flip extends the match toward THH; the other falls back to a shorter matched prefix (not always ∅).

feedback.correct: That's the THH system — solving it gives E₀ = 8.
hints: Every live state costs 1 per flip.
Split each row into the heads branch and the tails branch.
E₁ = 1 + ½·E₂ + ½·E₁; E₂ = 1 + ½·E₃ + ½·E₁.

beats.prompt: Solve both systems. What are the waits?

feedback.correct: THH = 8, HTH = 10 — the extra overlap costs HTH 2 more flips.
hints: THH has only the trivial overlap.
HTH has an extra length-1 overlap.
8 and 10.

beats.prompt: Run the simulation for THH. Does the empirical mean settle on 8?

feedback.correct: It converges to 8 — theory and simulation agree.
hints: Run a batch.
Watch the running mean settle.
It lands near 8.

beats.prompt: Add HTH's overlap chips. Each overlap length L drops a 2^L tile; the sum is the wait.

feedback.correct: 2¹ + 2³ = 2 + 8 = 10 — the border sum is the wait.
hints: One chip per overlap you found.
Lengths 1 and 3 → 2 and 8.
Add them: 10.

beats.prompt: What's the single difference that makes HTH wait longer than THH?

options.label: HTH keeps a matched H on a near-miss; THH doesn't

feedback.correct: Exactly — HTH's extra length-1 overlap lets a near-miss keep progress, which (paradoxically) lengthens the wait.
hints: Look at the near-miss edges side by side.
One pattern preserves a matched head; the other resets.
HTH's extra overlap is the difference.

beats.prompt: What you transferred to brand-new patterns.

feedback.correct: You built, solved, and overlap-checked two unseen patterns: THH = 8, HTH = 10.
hints: Same toolkit, new patterns.
Overlap sets the extra wait.
Done.
